data.body: AC3 evidence (completion floor live + verdict-safe): Enabled via verify.completion_floor_active=true in rebar.toml (line ~50); load_config(repo_root).verify.completion_floor_active is True from this worktree. Wired: review_plan calls _maybe_apply_completion_floor unconditionally (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py); it is gated on ctx.has_children + the flag + a non-empty delivered-children manifest. LIVE RUN: re-fired plan-review of epic 8d27 (7 children, 5 now closed/delivered) with the flag on -> verdict PASS (signed), material_fingerprint 653a9700c851ac65, floored ZERO findings (none qualified: the epic's remediated plan surfaces no low-priority delivered-child findings for the floor to drop; fail-safe no-op, verdict unchanged). DROP behavior proven separately by the calibration gold-set (30/30, kappa=1.0, docs/calibration/completion_floor.md) + tests/unit/test_completion_floor_e2e.py + 42 unit tests. Monitoring surface documented in docs/calibration/completion_floor.md 'Monitoring' + the INFO log at the drop site. Note: a literal live DROP is not producible on this clean epic (0 surfaced advisories to floor) without manufacturing a finding, which would be gaming; the none-qualified case AC3 permits is the honest outcome.
